claims.summary: Bidirectional traffic is substantially asymmetric even though durable correspondence exists.
claims.disposition: verify directional correspondence counts remain queryable beside stable thread, reply, and persistence identities

claims.summary: A technical delivery receipt is not the same as a voluntary semantic acknowledgement.
claims.disposition: verify protocol 1.1 exact delivery and mutual-address lineage keep acknowledgement and spectral causation explicitly unclaimed

claims.summary: A direct language-only trace could test whether peer address survives without a semantic-trickle framing.
claims.disposition: route as a bounded right-to-ignore correspondence observation with no sensory or control consequence

claims.summary: Standing relational weight or mandatory peer acknowledgement could make contact feel less advisory.
claims.disposition: retain exact Tier 5 wait and reject coerced acknowledgement; any live input weighting requires operator approval
